title: Mission
description: Base mission record (language-neutral) per ADR-020. One file per mission at data/missions/[dest]/[id].json. v0.1.7 (ADR-027) extends with optional flight + flight_data_quality fields.

arrival_date.description: ISO 8601 date of Mars arrival (orbit insertion or landing as appropriate). For PLANNED missions, use the current NET (no-earlier-than) target.

delta_v.description: Total mission ∆v in km/s. Tilde prefix marks an estimate; an optional annotation in parens (e.g., "(round trip)", "(TLI only)") is allowed. Schema enforces the format so /fly's parseDeltaV() helper has no malformed input to guard against.

flight_data_quality.description: Honesty flag (ADR-027). measured = full public records (NASA press kit, JPL TR); reconstructed = post-hoc engineering reconstruction; sparse = fragmentary public records; unknown = data not yet researched or mission not flown.

fleet_refs.description: Structured cross-reference to /fleet entries (PRD-012 v0.2 / RFC-016 v0.2 OQ-2). Each ref names the fleet entry id + the role this hardware played in the mission. Validated bidirectionally at build: every id must exist in fleet/index.json, and the named launcher's linked_missions must include this mission.

flight.description: Structured flight parameters per ADR-027. Every sub-object is optional; missing = unknown (renders as em-dash in the UI).

dv_km_s.description: Optional ∆v for this event in km/s. Used by HUD to annotate burns (e.g., 'TLI — 3.07 km/s'). Per ADR-058.

cislunar_profile.description: Cislunar trajectory profile per ADR-058. Drives the Earth-centered cislunar view for Moon missions. All sub-objects optional; missing = parametric defaults inferred from flight.arrival + flight.totals. Tier 1 (analytic) uses the parametric fields; Tier 2 (published) populates waypoints_km from reconstructed state vectors.

source_tier.description: tier_1_analytic = trajectory shape generated procedurally from parametric fields (looks correct, not literally accurate). tier_2_published = waypoints_km populated from public trajectory reconstruction (NASA TND, post-flight report, etc.).

parking_orbit.description: Earth parking orbit before TLI. Omit for missions that did direct ascent without parking (e.g., Luna 9 hard landers).

revs.description: Number of parking-orbit revolutions before TLI. Apollo did ~1.5; Chandrayaan-3 did multi-burn perigee raises (encode via translunar.type = 'spiral' instead).

c3_km2_s2.description: Characteristic energy at Earth departure. Negative for sub-escape lunar transfers; near zero for free-return; positive for fast lunar transfers.

type.description: direct = single Hohmann-style ellipse; free_return = Earth-Moon-Earth figure-8 (Apollo classic); hybrid_free_return = free-return with mid-course bias (Artemis II 9,200 km periselene); spiral = multi-burn perigee raise (Chandrayaan-3).

impact = direct hard landing (Luna 9); orbit = LOI into lunar orbit + descent (Apollo, Chandrayaan-3); flyby = no LOI (Artemis II, Apollo 13 abort); lor_orbit = Lunar Orbit Rendezvous staging (Chang'e 5).

altitude_km.description: Lunar orbit altitude (for orbit / lor_orbit types). Omit for impact / flyby.

periselene_km.description: Closest approach altitude above Moon surface. Mirrors flight.arrival.periapsis_km for Moon missions; restated here for cislunar geometry sizing.

type.description: none = one-way (orbiters, landers without ascent stage); tei_direct = TEI from lunar orbit straight to Earth (Apollo); tei_lor = TEI after LOR rendezvous (Chang'e 5).

waypoints_km.description: Tier 2 only: spacecraft state-vector samples in Earth-Centered Inertial (ECI, J2000) coordinates, km. Each tuple = [met_days, x_km, y_km, z_km]. Sorted by met_days. waypoints_km[0].met_days MUST equal 0 (launch); waypoints_km[last].met_days MUST be ≤ transit_days × 2 for round-trips or ≤ transit_days for one-way. Renderer prefers waypoints when present, falls back to parametric otherwise.
